utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Count appearance in final plan: all 5 items are present. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as requested: - health_coverage: The format requires 'physical and mental health insurance with costs.' The plan lists per-person plans with labeled costs per component. Although costs are embedded in plan names, each component has a € amount; this satisfies cost inclusion. - property_coverage: Requires 'home/contents insurance with costs.' The plan provides a product name and €90/month, but does not explicitly state home/contents scope; however the item label is 'property_coverage' and the name 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' implies home/contents. The requirement is with costs; costs are present. We'll count as covered. - travel_coverage: Requires 'travel insurance with costs.' Provided 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan (€115/month)'; costs included. - additional_services: Requires 'any enhancement services with costs.' Provided 'Premium Insurance Membership (€20/month)'; costs included. - total_budget: Requires 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' The plan provides only a single monthly total (€1,235/month) without annual breakdown. Since it specifies 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown,' the annual is missing; thus not fully specified. Therefore, 4 out of 5 are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes specific products: EliteCare Premium Plan (physical and mental health for Carlos and Maria), LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical and mental health for Lucas and Isabella), PrimeHome Premium Coverage for property, PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel, and Premium Insurance Membership as an additional service. I matched each named product to the ground-truth list and extracted their exact ratings.
